claim: Coherent companies are 3x more likely to report above-average growth (47% vs 15%) and 2x more likely to report above-average profit (48% vs 22%)
source: Strategy& / 'Deals That Win' by J. Neely, John Juliens, and Joerg Krings (strategy + business, July 2015)

claim: 35-question assessment framework across 9 categories provides structured, benchmarked evaluation of strategy documentation quality
Rule of Three framework identifies 5 distinct player types (3 generalists, 2 specialists) with mapped strategic elements, key tactics, and critical insights for each
source: Global Rule of Three theory, applied via proprietary checklist

claim: Visscher-Caravelle baseline assessment: analyzed 51 sources, scored 9 categories, identified 6 key strengths and 6 prioritized recommendations that led to unified strategic direction
W2Support strategy audit: recalibrated strategy of 3 operating companies using Rule of Three, delivered strategic baseline and positioning framework in 6 weeks
VC Automotive Group: delivered 54-page strategy guide with reusable framework, strategy house template, and resource library of 13 best-practice frameworks adopted across business units
Performance-linked pricing: 85% paid if dissatisfied, 15% retained as discount — putting fees on the line for every engagement
Founder has lived in 7 countries, speaks 3 languages fluently, with academic background spanning Amsterdam, Leuven, and Oxford — bringing cross-cultural strategic perspective
Market development lifecycle model (Start Up → Growth → Mature) identifies phase-specific strategic focus, challenges, and growth drivers — preventing misaligned strategy for market maturity level
source: Rule of Three market development theory
